pr.title: Match 11 functions across stage and fighter TUs
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: Matched and improved functions across fighter, stage, and interface TUs, replacing placeholders with evidence-backed C for CPU attack selection, match status dispatch, capture cleanup, and Ice Mountain/Pokemon Stadium/Fourside stage helpers. The PR also refined headers for newly understood signatures in `ftcpuattack.h` and `gricemt.h`; the automated decomp.dev report recorded +2388 matched-code bytes, +24 matched-data bytes, 11 new matches, and 6 additional unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.role: Added typed `ftCo_AttackEntry` layout and implemented `ftCo_800B6208` weighted random CPU attack command selection.
Forward-declared `struct ftCo_AttackEntry` and changed `ftCo_800B6208` from `UNK_T` to `struct ftCo_AttackEntry*`.
Added/improved fighter common helpers including `ftCo_800D72A0`, `ftCo_800D74A4`, and matched `fn_800DB6C8` capture cleanup.
Implemented `ifStatus_802F7034` dispatch from the `gm_8016AE38()` status block to `ifStatus_802F6EA4`.
Matched several Ice Mountain helpers, including joint translation init, wave/cloud update, material refresh loops, and dirty-bit joint rebinding.
Corrected `grIceMt_801F98A8` to return `void` and expanded `grIceMt_801F9ACC` to include callback/event and ground object parameters.
Improved Pokemon Stadium init/model setup, render callback, toon texture setup, transformation state fields, and vertex update loop details.
Fixed `grFourside_801F2F34` callback registration by passing the ground GObj to `mpJointSetCb1` instead of the inner `Ground*`.

what_changed: `ftCo_800B6208` replaced a placeholder with a null-terminated weighted-selection loop over `ftCo_AttackEntry`, using `HSD_Randf()`, a near-zero sum guard, and an `__assert` fallback.

`fn_800DB6C8` was implemented as a captured-pulled-hi cleanup hook: it selects `fn_800DB790` or `fn_800DBAE4` by motion state, clears victim-specific attached items for Yoshi, Link/CLink, and Samus, then calls `ftCommon_8007EBAC`.
`ifStatus_802F7034` now normalizes status-block indices from `gm_8016AE38()` and selects an `ifStatus_802F6EA4` mode from `unk_B`, `match_result`, and `x24C8.x5_1`.
`grIceMt_801F72D4` now uses separate `Vec3` locals and a static `grIm_803B8220` lookup to seed joint translations for joints `0x12` through `0x15`.
`grIceMt_801F7728` was updated to compute a vertical effect offset and call `grIceMt_801F9ACC` with the fuller signature, event callback, and ground object argument.
`grIceMt_801F7F1C` and `grIceMt_801F81B4` now iterate the two cached `gp->gv.icemt.xF8[]` materials and call `grMaterial_801C8CDC` only when non-null.
`grIceMt_801F98A8` changed from a speculative integer/bool-returning helper to a `void` dirty-bit callback that clears `xC4.b1` and rebinds up to four joints via `Ground_801C2D0C`.
`grPura_80212024` was improved to set `x11_flags.b012`, choose distinct current/next Pokemon Stadium transformation indices with `HSD_Randi(4)`, and update camera/background colors.
`grPura_80212290` now sets the render callback, applies the toon texture image by address, raises the `0x1000` JObj flag, resets toon texture to `NULL`, and starts idle animation.
Adjacent Pokemon Stadium fixes changed cached transform-joint storage from `gp->gv.pura.xC8` to `gp->gv.pura2.xC8` and adjusted the `grPu_803E6C0C` loop stride to `+= 6` u16 entries.
`grFourside_801F2F34` corrected a one-argument mismatch in `mpJointSetCb1(0, gobj, grFourside_801F30A0)`, which the automated report showed as enough to reach 100% for that function.
smart_moves: Refined function signatures only where the new bodies forced them: `ftCo_800B6208` gained a typed attack-entry pointer, `grIceMt_801F98A8` became `void`, and `grIceMt_801F9ACC` gained its callback and ground-object parameters.
Used minimal struct recovery for `ftCo_AttackEntry`: kept only known fields (`cmd` at `+0x00`, `weight` at `+0x18`) and explicit padding to preserve the `0x24` stride.
Matched codegen-sensitive stack behavior with `PAD_STACK`, `UNUSED` stack bytes, separate `Vec3` locals, and `#pragma dont_inline` around `fn_800DB6C8`.
Corrected callback object identity rather than forcing casts everywhere: stage callbacks such as `mpJointSetCb1` and `grIceMt_801F9ACC` needed the GObj/Ground_GObj, not the inner `Ground*`.
Left weakly understood fields as offset-style names (`x2D0`, `xC4`, `xF8`, `unk_10`, `unk_B`) while still improving type safety around confirmed structures and callbacks.
decomp_lessons: A speculative callback return type can block matching; if the body has no return, update the header to `void` instead of preserving an assumed `bool`.

Stage union field selection matters for both type correctness and matching: this PR used fields such as `gp->gv.icemt.xF8[]`, `gp->gv.icemt2.xC4`, and `gp->gv.pura2.xC8` where the previous guesses were wrong or too generic.
Small one-line pointer fixes can matter late in a function: passing `gobj` instead of `gp` to `mpJointSetCb1` moved `grFourside_801F2F34` from near-match to 100%.
Weighted table functions are good candidates for local padded typedefs when the sentinel, stride, and meaningful fields are known but the full structure is not.
For material/collision/stage helpers, null checks against cached arrays and exact loop form can be more match-relevant than the high-level behavior.
When a callee signature is discovered from one matched call site, update the header so later TUs reuse the same ABI evidence instead of duplicating casts or `UNK_T`.
naming_conventions: Stage symbols kept established prefixes: `grIceMt_` for Ice Mountain, `grPura_` for Pokemon Stadium, `grFourside_` for Fourside, with globals such as `grIm_803B8220` and `grPu_803E6AA0`.
Fighter common symbols retained the `ftCo_` prefix; local/static unknown helpers remained address-based `fn_800D...` names where semantics were not strong enough for a rename.
Kind and motion constants followed existing conventions such as `FTKIND_YOSHI`, `FTKIND_LINK`, `FTKIND_CLINK`, `FTKIND_SAMUS`, and `ftCo_MS_CapturePulledHi`.
The recovered table type was named `ftCo_AttackEntry`, matching the `ftCo_` subsystem prefix while avoiding over-specific naming beyond the known CPU attack-selection use.
Unknown fields were not over-renamed; offset-style names like `xC4`, `xC6`, `xC8`, `xF8`, `x223C`, and `unk_10` stayed in place.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(0x14)`, `PAD_STACK(0x10)`, `PAD_STACK(16)`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, and `UNUSED unsigned char _[8]` to reproduce expected stack frames.
Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around `fn_800DB6C8` to prevent codegen from inlining a matched helper.
Made `grIm_803B8220` `static` and used four separate `Vec3` locals in `grIceMt_801F72D4`, reflecting linkage and stack-layout sensitivity.
Used exact callback/cast shape in Ice Mountain: `grIceMt_801F9ACC((HSD_GObj*) &gp->gv.corneria.xC4, ..., fn_801F9038, gobj)`.
Used `UnkFlagStruct` bitfield access for `gp->gv.icemt2.xC4.b1` instead of a raw byte mask in `grIceMt_801F98A8`.
Used `M2C_FIELD` accesses and changed the `grPura_80213030` pointer increment from `+= 0xC` to `+= 6` u16 elements to better match the original loop.
Verification evidence in the PR body cited `ninja` producing `build-linux/GALE01/main.dol: OK`, per-function `objdiff-cli diff` at 100%, and no fuzzy regressions.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
The automated decomp.dev comment reported matched code at 65.89% (+0.06%, +2388 bytes) and matched data at 38.70% (+24 bytes).

The same automated report listed partial improvements, not full matches, for `ftCo_800D74A4` at 86.93%, `ftCo_800D72A0` at 97.04%, `grPura_80212024` at 93.58%, `ftcpuattack` `.sdata2` at 80.45%, `grPura_80213030` at 95.47%, and `grIceMt_801F9ACC` at 18.88%.
searchable_terms: Match 11 functions across stage and fighter TUs
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish `ftCo_800D72A0`, `ftCo_800D74A4`, and `grPura_80212024`, since the automated report still showed them below 100%.
Search for other `mpJointSetCb1` call sites that might incorrectly pass `Ground*` instead of the stage GObj.
Audit other callers of `grIceMt_801F9ACC` after the expanded signature to confirm all call sites agree on `HSD_GObjEvent` and `Ground_GObj*` parameters.
Look for additional uses of the `ftCo_AttackEntry` table layout; promote the typedef out of `ftcpuattack.c` if reused across TUs.
Investigate `lbl_8046B6A0_t` fields used by `ifStatus_802F7034` (`unk_10`, `unk_14`, `unk_18`, `unk_B`, `match_result`) for better names once more status code is matched.
Compare other Ice Mountain material-refresh helpers against the `gp->gv.icemt.xF8[]` loop pattern from `grIceMt_801F7F1C` and `grIceMt_801F81B4`.
